description: allocate_port (bin/devenv:167-172) calls bare python3 which is not installed in the devenv container. Function always returns 1, causing SSH port allocation to silently fail. Rewrite to use 'uv run python3 -c ...' per project convention. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #6.
title: Fix allocate_port: rewrite to use uv run python3

description:
Create tests/bats/log_primitives.bats with tests for shared/bash/log.sh. Test scenarios:

- log_debug with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG: message appears on stderr in format [timestamp] [DEBUG  ] <msg>.
- log_debug with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING (default): no output on stderr.
- log_info with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=INFO: message appears on stderr.
- log_info with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING (default): suppressed — no output.
- log_warning with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING: message appears on stderr.
- log_warning with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=INFO: message appears (WARNING >= INFO).
- log_warning with DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=ERROR: suppressed (WARNING < ERROR).
- log_error with any level: message always appears on stderr (ERROR is highest).
- die: exits with code 1; message on stderr in ERROR format.
- Log output format: matches pattern \[.*\] \[.*\] <message> — do NOT assert timestamps.

Key constraints:
(1) DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL is declared readonly at log.sh:16. Tests that vary the log level MUST set DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL BEFORE sourcing log.sh.
(2) _DEVENV_LOG_SH_SOURCED is also readonly (log.sh:5-8). This guard prevents re-sourcing. Since unsetting a readonly variable fails, each test that needs a different log level must run in a FRESH SUBSHELL that sources log.sh independently: `bash -c 'export DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG; source shared/bash/log.sh; log_debug "test msg"'`.
(3) Spec says LOG_LEVEL (line 138) but implementation uses DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL — devenv-8x0 fixes this spec bug. Tests use the actual variable name.

See research/testing-standard/research.md for full scenario table.
title: Write log_primitives.bats — log level filtering and die tests

description: Create tests/bats/build_devenv_cli.bats. Prerequisites: fake docker handles 'docker info' (validate_docker at main() line 261). Test setup strategy: point DEVENV_HOME to the real repo root so Dockerfiles exist at their expected paths (docker/base/Dockerfile.base, docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv, shared/tools/Dockerfile.${tool}). This avoids creating stub Dockerfiles and tests against real file layout. Scenarios: no args (exit 1, Usage: from usage()), --stage no value (exit 1), --stage invalid (exit 1), --stage base (exit 0, fake docker log shows 'build' with '-f .../docker/base/Dockerfile.base'), --tool jq (exit 0, fake docker log shows 'build' with '-f .../shared/tools/Dockerfile.jq'), --tool nonexistent (exit 1, 'Tool Dockerfile not found'), --tool tree-sitter (dependency chain — fake docker 'images' must return 'tools-node:latest' for the dependency check; requires both Dockerfile.tree-sitter and Dockerfile.node), --project /nonexistent (exit 1, 'does not exist'), --unknown (exit 1, 'Unknown option'). Also includes independent unit tests for duplicated primitives: resolve_project_path (line 64) and derive_project_image_suffix (line 85) from bin/build-devenv, tested separately from bin/devenv copies per spec section 4. Use regex for die output: .*\[ERROR.*\].*<message>.*. See research/testing-standard/research.md.
title: Write build_devenv_cli.bats — argument parsing and docker intent tests

description:
Create tests/bats/devenv_primitives.bats. Source bin/devenv (BASH_SOURCE guard prevents main execution). Test 10 primitives:

1. resolve_project_path: '.' resolves to PWD; absolute path passed through; relative path resolved; nonexistent path returns 1.
2. resolve_container_project_path: path under HOME maps correctly; path outside HOME triggers die — test in subshell via `bash -c 'source bin/devenv; resolve_container_project_path /outside'` to capture exit code 1 and stderr message (cannot use bats 'run' directly since die calls exit and function is sourced).
3. derive_container_name: standard path produces 'devenv-parent-basename'; special chars sanitized; empty result returns 1.
4. derive_project_label: produces 'parent/basename'.
5. derive_project_image_suffix: sanitized 'parent-basename'; special chars replaced; empty returns 1.
6. is_valid_port: valid (1, 22, 65535); invalid (0, 65536, 'abc', '', negative).
7. allocate_port: after devenv-032 lands, assert numeric output 1-65535; until then, assert exit code 1 (bare python3 not installed).
8. get_image_name: without .devenv/Dockerfile returns 'devenv:latest'; with it returns 'devenv-project-<suffix>:latest'.
9. build_mounts: populates array with mandatory volume mounts; SSH_AUTH_SOCK conditional; config file conditionals (create .gitconfig, .ssh/authorized_keys in temp HOME to test conditional mount paths, otherwise conditionals are skipped). Uses nameref (local -n) — requires bash 4.3+. Caller must pass variable name, declare array, call function, assert array contents.
10. build_env_vars: populates array with TERM; SSH_AUTH_SOCK conditional.

See research/testing-standard/research.md Function/Block-Level Detail.
title: Write devenv_primitives.bats — unit tests for 10 primitives

description:
Create the full test directory tree per spec: tests/bats/, tests/e2e-human/, tests/helpers/, tests/fixtures/bin/. Create two helper files:

tests/helpers/test_env.bash — setup/teardown used by every .bats file:
- setup: Create temp HOME (mktemp -d), set HOME, set LC_ALL=C, set PATH to tests/fixtures/bin:$DEVENV_HOME/bin:$DEVENV_HOME/scripts:/usr/bin:/bin, export DEVENV_HOME pointing to repo root.
- setup must also ensure fake docker handles 'docker info' (validate_docker is called at main() entry in both bin/devenv and bin/build-devenv — this should be built into the helper, not handled per-test).
- teardown: Remove temp HOME directory.

tests/helpers/assert_output.bash — assertion helpers:
- assert_exit_code() — compare $status against expected value.
- assert_stdout_contains() — grep $output for substring.
- assert_stderr_contains() — grep stderr capture for substring.
- assert_stdout_empty() / assert_stderr_empty().
- refute_stdout_contains() — inverse.
- Output normalization: helpers should trim trailing whitespace before comparison (spec line 83: 'Normalize outputs before assertion'). Do NOT assert timestamps — use regex patterns that ignore timestamp fields.

See research/testing-standard/research.md File-Level Change Map.
title: Create test directory structure and helpers

description: Create tests/bats/install_devenv_cli.bats. Prerequisites: DEVENV_HOME must point to directory with bin/build-devenv and bin/devenv (or create stubs — install_devenv() checks these files exist at lines 54-59). Scenarios: --help (exit 0, Usage:), default install (creates symlinks in temp HOME/.local/bin/, exit 0, correct symlink targets), idempotent install (run twice succeeds; existing symlinks removed and recreated), --uninstall (removes symlinks, exit 0), --uninstall already uninstalled (exit 0; requires DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=INFO to assert 'Symlink not found' since log_info is suppressed at default WARNING level), --source /custom/path (custom symlink targets), --source no value (exit 1), --unknown (exit 1). Test must use temp HOME so $HOME/.local/bin/ is isolated. Verify symlink targets point to correct source files. Clean up in teardown. See research/testing-standard/research.md.
title: Write install_devenv_cli.bats — install/uninstall contract tests

description: bd doctor's Git Hooks check reports hooks as @unknown/0.0.0 and incompatible with Dolt backend, even immediately after running bd hooks install --force. This is a known upstream bug (steveyegge/beads#2244) fixed in commit b96483fc. The fix teaches parseBDHookVersion() and CheckGitHooksDoltCompatibility() to recognize the section-marker hook format. Resolution: upgrade bd past commit b96483fc.
title: bd doctor reports hook incompatibility even after bd hooks install --force

description: specs/testing-standard.md line 138 references LOG_LEVEL but the implementation in shared/bash/log.sh:15 uses DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL. The code is correct (namespaced to avoid collisions). Update the spec to match the implementation. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #5.
title: Fix spec bug: LOG_LEVEL should be DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL in testing-standard.md

description: bats-core is not installed in the devenv container. Add it to the devenv Dockerfile (via shared/tools/ or docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv) so it's always available. This aligns with the project pattern for tooling. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #1. This is a prerequisite for all test files.
title: Install bats-core in devenv image

description:
Epic: Implement the testing standard defined in specs/testing-standard.md. The codebase currently has zero test coverage, no test infrastructure (bats-core, helpers, fixtures), and two bugs identified during gap analysis. See research/testing-standard/research.md for full gap analysis (26 gaps: 2 critical, 3 high, 4 medium, 2 low).

Implementation order (strict — each phase depends on the previous):

Phase 0 — Fix critical bugs (prerequisites for correct test design):
  - devenv-8x0: Fix spec bug (LOG_LEVEL -> DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL in testing-standard.md)
  - devenv-032: Fix allocate_port (rewrite to use uv run python3)

Phase 1 — Infrastructure (no tests can run without these):
  - devenv-bzy: Install bats-core in devenv image
  - devenv-7v1: Create test directory structure and helpers (test_env.bash, assert_output.bash)
  - devenv-geg: Create fake docker fixture

Phase 2 — Unit/primitive tests (testing trophy: many, fast, no docker):
  - devenv-4tk: Write log_primitives.bats (depends on devenv-8x0)
  - devenv-75m: Write devenv_primitives.bats (depends on devenv-032)

Phase 3 — CLI contract tests (testing trophy: some, fake docker):
  - devenv-i2d: Write devenv_cli.bats
  - devenv-592: Write build_devenv_cli.bats
  - devenv-7v8: Write install_devenv_cli.bats

Phase 4 — Static analysis + verification:
  - devenv-yst: Create shellcheck runner
  - devenv-osi: Verify full test suite passes without Docker (final gate)

Phase 5 — Low priority / future:
  - devenv-jje: Create e2e-human test placeholders
  - devenv-pzg: Extract shared primitives to common library

Key cross-cutting concerns:
- DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL defaults to WARNING — tests asserting log_info messages must set DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=INFO explicitly.
- DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL and _DEVENV_LOG_SH_SOURCED are readonly — tests must use fresh subshells per log level.
- validate_docker is pervasive — fake docker 'info' handling built into test_env.bash, not per-test.
- die wraps output in log format — use regex patterns .*\[ERROR.*\].*<message>.* for assertions.
- Duplicated primitives (resolve_project_path, derive_project_image_suffix) tested independently per spec section 4.
title: Implement Testing Standard

description: Create tests/fixtures/bin/docker — executable bash script that: logs all invocations (full argument list) to DOCKER_LOG or $BATS_TMPDIR/docker_calls.log, returns exit 0 by default, supports FAKE_DOCKER_EXIT for exit code override, supports FAKE_DOCKER_OUTPUT for canned stdout. Must handle subcommands: ps, stop, run, exec, build, info, images, volume, system. Key requirements: (1) 'docker info' must return 0 — both bin/devenv and bin/build-devenv call validate_docker at main() entry; this is a prerequisite for ALL CLI tests. (2) 'docker ps' must support --format flag for output column selection — list and stop tests depend on formatted output. (3) 'docker ps -q --filter volume=...' must support returning non-empty output for volume rm --all in-use tests. (4) 'docker images' must support returning image names for build-devenv dependency chain tests (tree-sitter requires tools-node:latest). Start minimal and add subcommand-specific handling as tests require it. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #4.
title: Create fake docker fixture

description: Create tests/bats/devenv_cli.bats. CLI contract tests running bin/devenv as executable with fake docker on PATH. Prerequisites: fake docker handles 'docker info' for validate_docker (handled by test_env.bash setup). Scenarios: devenv list (exit 0, NAME/SSH/STATUS/STARTED columns), devenv stop --all with no containers (exit 0; requires DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL=INFO to assert 'No devenv containers running' since log_info is suppressed at default WARNING level), devenv stop no target (exit 1, error), devenv volume rm no args (exit 1), devenv volume rm --all with in-use volume (exit 1; fake docker 'ps -q --filter volume=...' must return non-empty), devenv volume no subcommand (exit 1), devenv volume list (exit 0; fake docker receives 'volume ls --filter label=devenv=true'), devenv volume rm --force (exit 0; skips confirmation), devenv volume rm with 'echo y' piped (exit 0; prints prompt to stderr), devenv help (exit 0, Usage:). Use regex patterns for die output: .*\[ERROR.*\].*<message>.* — do NOT assert timestamps. See research/testing-standard/research.md.
title: Write devenv_cli.bats — CLI contract tests for devenv

description: Create tests/e2e-human/README.md documenting the opt-in Docker E2E testing process and tests/e2e-human/devenv_e2e.bats as a minimal placeholder. These are low-priority items that document how to run integration tests that require real Docker. See research/testing-standard/research.md gaps #11 and #12.
title: Create e2e-human test placeholders

description:
Final verification gate for epic devenv-dk8. This task validates all acceptance criteria from specs/testing-standard.md lines 140-146.

Verification steps:
1. Run `bats tests/bats/` — all tests must pass. e2e-human/ is excluded (opt-in only).
2. Inspect DOCKER_LOG (or $BATS_TMPDIR/docker_calls.log) — confirm only fake docker invocations, no real docker calls. The test suite must succeed even without Docker installed/running.
3. Verify tests are fast (seconds, not minutes) and deterministic — run suite 3 times, all passes.
4. Run `shellcheck bin/devenv bin/build-devenv scripts/install-devenv shared/bash/log.sh` — zero warnings.
5. Confirm CLI contract coverage: exit codes, stdout, stderr, and docker intent are tested for all scripts.
6. Confirm primitive tests exist only for explicitly stable, reusable primitives (per spec section 4).

Success criteria: steps 1-6 all pass. This blocks epic completion — do not close devenv-dk8 until this task is done.

See research/testing-standard/research.md Suggested Implementation Order steps 12-13.
title: Verify full test suite passes without Docker

description: resolve_project_path exists in both bin/devenv (line 87) and bin/build-devenv (line 64). derive_project_image_suffix exists in both bin/devenv (line 150) and bin/build-devenv (line 85). The spec says to test each copy independently 'until the duplication is resolved by extracting a shared library.' This task is to extract the duplicated primitives to a shared bash library (e.g., shared/bash/paths.sh). Lower priority — tests should be written first per spec guidance. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #3.
title: Extract shared primitives to common library

description: shellcheck v0.11.0 is installed but there's no runner script or CI integration. Create a way to run shellcheck on all scripts (bin/devenv, bin/build-devenv, scripts/install-devenv, shared/bash/log.sh) and verify zero warnings. This could be a simple shell script or documented run command. See research/testing-standard/research.md gap #17.
title: Create shellcheck runner and verify all scripts pass
